items.q: What are the three primary colours in paint (subtractive)?
items.correct: Red, yellow, and blue (or magenta, yellow, cyan in print).
items.why: Students confuse light (additive: RGB) with paint/print (subtractive: RYB or CMY). RGB mixes light to white; RYB/CMY mixes pigment toward black - different colour models, different primaries.

items.q: What did Renaissance artists rediscover that medieval art lacked?
items.correct: Linear perspective and naturalistic representation of the human body.
items.why: Students assume medieval art was colourless. It was vivid but flat - the Renaissance breakthrough was mathematical PERSPECTIVE (Brunelleschi) and anatomical realism, not the introduction of colour.

items.q: What were Impressionist painters primarily trying to capture?
items.correct: The fleeting effects of light and atmosphere at a particular moment.
items.misconception: Their feelings or first impressions of a scene.
items.why: Students take 'impression' as personal feeling. Impressionism was about visual perception - how light actually appears on surfaces, painted quickly outdoors before light changed.
It's Cloud. “Pentagon” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many sides on an octagon?
items.why: The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
